Updated 06/03/2004

PROGRAM
The Second Annual Alpha Project Research Symposium
"Predicting the Quantitative Behavior of the Yeast Response to Mating Pheromone"
June 4, 2004
Download PDF of the program.
Download PDF of Poster Session abstracts.

8:00 am - 9:00 am Registration and Coffee

9:00 am - 9:15 am Opening Remarks: Welcome; Alpha Project history and overview
Roger Brent, President and Research Director
The Molecular Sciences Institute

9:15 am - 10:00 am KEYNOTE "Global mapping of the yeast genetic interaction network"
Charles Boone, Department of Medical Genetics & Microbiology
University of Toronto

10:00 am - 10:30 am "Progress toward a list of pathway specs"
Kirsten Benjamin
The Molecular Sciences Institute

10:30 am - 10:45 am Break

10:45 am - 11:15 am "Monod, natural language organizer and communications system"
David Soergel
The Molecular Sciences Institute

11:15 am - 12:00 pm "Noisy machines: In vivo biochemistry for genetic networks"
Michael Elowitz, Assistant Professor of Biology & Applied Physics
California Institute of Technology

12:00 pm - 1:00 pm Lunch Break

1:00 pm - 1:30 pm "Mechanisms controlling cell-to-cell variation in pheromone response"
Alejandro Colman-Lerner
The Molecular Sciences Institute

1:30 pm - 1:45 pm "Grabbing quantitative information for single cells and what I have learned working with biologists"
Andrew Gordon
The Molecular Sciences Institute

1:45 pm - 2:00 pm "Quantifying the dynamics of downstream pathway events"
Richard Yu
The Molecular Sciences Institute

2:00 pm - 2:30 pm "Creation of tadpole methods to detect and count small numbers of molecules"
Ian Burbulis
The Molecular Sciences Institute

2:30 pm - 2:45 pm Break

2:45 pm - 3:10 pm "A calculus for biology"
Jehoshua Bruck
Gordon and Betty Moore Professor of Computation and Neural Systems & Electrical Engineering
California Institute of Technology

3:15 pm - 3:45 pm "Design of genetic systems"
Drew Endy
Massachusetts Institute of Technology

3:45 pm - 4:15 pm "Finding new pathway connections and summary of where project stands"
Orna Resnekov, Alpha Project Manager & Assistant Research Director
The Molecular Sciences Institute

4:15 pm - 4:30 pm Challenges and closing thoughts
Roger Brent, President and Research Director
The Molecular Sciences Institute

Reception & Poster Session
